target: Part 2 (Self-Contradictions), globe-in-disguise fingerprint narrative — append vertical fingerprint paragraph
source_note: analyst-v51.1-analysis; companion to the existing horizontal fingerprint paragraph (a = πR_Earth)
current_text_location:
data/sections.json → part2 → search for '

But the parameters are the globe in disguise'
old_html: But the parameters are the globe in disguise. The disc semi-major axis a = 20,015 km is, within 11 km, the pole-to-pole surface distance on a WGS84 ellipsoid (half the polar circumference: 40,008 / 2 = 20,004 km). More revealing: a = π × REarth. Earth's mean radius is 6,371 km; multiply by π and you get 20,015.1 km — matching the dome's disc radius to within 114 meters (0.0006%). On a flat disc, the pole-to-rim distance is a straight radial line. There is no geometric reason a flat disc's radius should involve π. The factor π enters because this distance is actually the arc length from pole to antipode along a curved sphere: ∫₀π R dθ = πR. The dome's disc radius is not a measured flat-earth distance — it is a spherical surface integral in disguise. This is the layperson-accessible version of the technical argument: if the Earth were truly flat, why does the distance from pole to rim equal π times anything?
replacement_html:
But the parameters are the globe in disguise. The disc semi-major axis a = 20,015 km is, within 11 km, the pole-to-pole surface distance on a WGS84 ellipsoid (half the polar circumference: 40,008 / 2 = 20,004 km). More revealing: a = π × REarth. Earth's mean radius is 6,371 km; multiply by π and you get 20,015.1 km — matching the dome's disc radius to within 114 meters (0.0006%). On a flat disc, the pole-to-rim distance is a straight radial line. There is no geometric reason a flat disc's radius should involve π. The factor π enters because this distance is actually the arc length from pole to antipode along a curved sphere: ∫₀π R dθ = πR. The dome's disc radius is not a measured flat-earth distance — it is a spherical surface integral in disguise. This is the layperson-accessible version of the technical argument: if the Earth were truly flat, why does the distance from pole to rim equal π times anything?

The vertical fingerprint is even more damning: δ = REarth. The horizontal case above fixes the pole-to-rim distance. The dome also needs a vertical scale for its sub-surface structure, and the number it picks is, impossibly, the same globe radius. The V13 model page publishes the sub-terrestrial cavity formula Sub-H(r) = H(r) × (1 − e−r/δ) with δ = 6,371 km — literally the mean radius of the spherical Earth, to four-figure precision, adopted as a dome parameter with no derivation. The model page provides no flat-disc justification: not a ray-trace, not a dielectric argument, not even a narrative reason why a flat layer should have a depth scale that happens to equal the radius of a body the dome claims doesn't exist. It is simply stated. This is the smoking-gun companion to the horizontal fingerprint: both principal dome length scales — the pole-to-rim distance a and the sub-terrestrial depth δ — are explicit functions of REarth (6,371 km), with one factor of π for the arc-length case and nothing for the radial case. In a self-consistent flat-earth model, δ ought to be an independent fit parameter; instead it is the globe constant, copy-pasted. And the boundary the dome claims this depth scale explains — the seismic P-wave shadow zone at 104°–140° — is itself a spherical-shell refraction result: the angular cutoffs encode the core-to-mantle ratio 3,480/6,371 = 0.546 under Snell's law applied to concentric spheres. The dome offers no wave equation, no velocity profile, and no ray-tracing to show how a flat boundary could produce sharp angular cutoffs matching spherical refraction to better than 1%. Two independent globe constants, two dome scales, one fit: the horizontal (a = πR) and the vertical (δ = R) are both the globe in disguise.
holes_addressed: ISS-641 (EXP-042 tracker link): globe fingerprint narrative in Part 2 discussed only the horizontal a = πR_Earth case; the equally strong vertical δ = R_Earth case was missing
Part 2 previously allowed a reader to dismiss the πR coincidence as a single cherry-picked numerical collision; with δ = R_Earth as a second independent match, the 'coincidence' defense fails
WIN-064 (Sub-Terrestrial Wall) kernel was analysed in monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-064.json but never made it into the reader-facing Self-Contradictions prose
new_evidence_added: Dome model page V13 Sub-H(r) formula: Sub-H(r) = H(r) × (1 − e^{−r/δ}), δ = 6,371 km (source: john09289.github.io/predictions/model.html)

Spherical-shell P-wave shadow zone geometry: cutoffs at 104° and 140° encode the core/mantle ratio 3,480/6,371 ≈ 0.546 via Snell's law applied to concentric spheres (standard seismology, e.g. Stein & Wysession, An Introduction to Seismology, §3)
Earth mean radius R_Earth = 6,371.0 km (IUGG 1980; IERS Conventions 2010)
anticipated_objections: 'δ is a free parameter the dome happened to fit to 6,371 km' → Then the dome model page would publish a fit history or derivation; it publishes only the stated value. The V12/V13 evolution notes show no exploration of alternative δ values — unlike eccentricity e, which has a documented scan from 0.44 to 0.71.

'Lots of length scales are near 6,371 km by accident' → The probability that an independently-fit vertical depth scale lands within 0.1% of R_Earth while the horizontal scale simultaneously lands within 0.0006% of πR_Earth is effectively zero without shared provenance.

'Flat earth could still have that depth as a free choice' → Fine, but then the dome must either (a) derive it from flat-disc physics, or (b) admit it was copied. The V13 page does neither; it states δ = 6,371 km as a bare constant. Our criticism stands either way.

'The P-wave shadow zone match is numerical coincidence too' → The 104°–140° band is not a single number — it is a functional angular signature of Snell's law on concentric spheres. A flat layer cannot reproduce that signature regardless of the depth constant chosen.
kernel_of_truth: The V13 Sub-H(r) formula is a genuine structural addition to the dome model: it acknowledges that sub-surface behaviour needs its own depth scale, not just the firmament height H(r). Publishing an explicit functional form rather than a hand-wave is, by alternative-cosmology standards, unusually disciplined. The author deserves credit for writing the equation down and exposing δ as a named parameter — which is exactly what lets us identify it as the globe radius. Our criticism is not that the dome has no sub-terrestrial model; it is that the sub-terrestrial model's only dimensional input is R_Earth, a spherical quantity, in a flat-earth theory.
integration_notes:

Decider can apply this as a standard find-and-replace patch against data/sections.json → part2 → html. After integration, this EXP-042 closes the globe-fingerprint coordinate-system narrative hole called out in the expansion tracker note: 'Smoking-gun: both horizontal (a = πR) and vertical (δ = R) dome scales are globe-derived. Combine with existing disc radius fingerprint for devastating one-two punch.'
